explicit_text: I was born in Seattle in 1963, but we moved to Jersey shortly after. Seattle has always been important to me, especially since my family is from there. The music scene in Seattle has been tough lately, especially with the pandemic and protests impacting venues and clubs. I think the city's tech industry has a strong hold on it, which makes it challenging for musicians to find footing.

explicit_text: After finishing high school, I went to Berklee College of Music and graduated from there. Berklee was where I really honed my craft and prepared for a professional career in music. It was a pivotal time before I moved to the Bay Area and then New York City.

explicit_text: I moved to New York City around 1987 and have been based here ever since. New York has been central to my music career, though the COVID-19 pandemic and recent protests have drastically affected the scene. The shutdowns canceled all my gigs and tours, including a planned tour with Springsteen and one in Spain. Despite the challenges, I’m trying to adapt by doing remote recording and live-streamed shows.

explicit_text: The COVID-19 pandemic hit abruptly and changed everything for me and many musicians. Tours and gigs were canceled almost overnight, including my planned Spain tour and a tour with Springsteen. It has been devastating, with many friends getting sick or worse, and the music community struggling financially and emotionally. I’ve been focusing on keeping morale up and adapting by doing remote recording and live streaming.
